completed_work: Added additive validation.l0_invariants JSON output with ok/errors.
Added tests for valid validation output, invalid validation output with exit 0, invalid validation output with exit 1, and flag gating.
key_decisions: Keep CLI validation optional and limited to --print-context-summary --summary-format json.
Keep default JSON output unchanged unless --validate-l0-invariants is explicitly used.
Use --fail-on-invalid-l0 as the opt-in nonzero failure mode.
next_actions: Consider either structured validation error codes, L0 chunk JSON validation at create/load time, malformed L0 store diagnostics, or guarded explicit L0 context-pack inclusion.
risks: Validation errors currently expose message-only entries, not structured codes or fields.
Validation is still summary-layer only; it does not validate L0 chunk files.
summary_text: AI-Lab wired provider L0 summary invariant validation into CLI dry-run JSON summary paths. ask_provider.py and compare_providers.py now support --validate-l0-invariants for --print-context-summary --summary-format json, adding an additive validation.l0_invariants object with ok/errors. They also support --fail-on-invalid-l0 to return non-zero when validation fails. Without --validate-l0-invariants, default JSON output is unchanged. The feature reuses validate_provider_l0_invariants, remains read-only, and does not change prompt assembly, context packs, L0 creation/loading, or provider calls. Full suite passed with 242 tests.
